heading: Requisitions for advances and removal of charges outstanding in accounts of advances
label: 2777 Requisitions for advances and removal of charges outstanding in accounts of advances
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2777(a)31:536, 537.(1st, 2d pars. under heading “Advances to Disbursing Officers”), .June 5, 1920, ch. 24041 Stat. 97531:539, 540., §§ 1, 2, .June 19, 1878, ch. 31220 Stat. 1672777(b), (c)31:95b (related to Army, Navy, Air Force).(related to Army, Navy, Air Force), ; , , .June 4, 1954, ch. 264, § 168 Stat. 175Pub. L. 92–310, § 231(gg)86 Stat. 213June 6, 1972In the section, the words “disbursing official” are substituted for “disbursing officers” for consistency with other titles of the United States Code.In subsection (a), before clause (1), the words “Secretary of a military department” are substituted for “Secretary of the Army” in 31:536 and for “Secretary of the Navy” in 31:539 because of 10:101(7). The title of Secretary of War was changed to Secretary of the Army by (ch. 343, ), and by sections 1 and 53 of the Act of (ch. 1041, , 676). The Secretary of the Air Force is included because of sections 205(a) and 207(a) and (f) of the Act of (ch. 343, , 502), and (ch. 1041, ). In clause (1), the word “General” in 31:539 is omitted as surplus. In clause (3), the words “and ‘Pay of the Navy’ shall be used only for its legitimate purpose, as provided by law” are omitted as unnecessary. In clause (5), the words “by pay and counterwarrant” in 31:537 and 540 are omitted as unnecessary.section 205(a) of the Act of July 26, 194761 Stat. 50170A Stat. 15761 Stat. 501section 1 of the Act of August 10, 195670A Stat. 488August 10, 1956July 26, 1947In subsection (b), before clause (1), the word “appropriate” is omitted as surplus. The words “deducting the amount of the charge from” are substituted for “debiting” for clarity. In clause (2)(B), the word “concerned” is omitted as surplus.In subsection (c), the words “in any way” and “of the United States” are omitted as surplus.

Amendments1996—Subsec. (b)(2)(B). struck out “to the Comptroller General” after “head of the department”.Pub. L. 104–3161984—Subsec. (c). struck out “of this section” after “Subsection (b)”.Pub. L. 98–525

children.text: under an “account of advances” for the department;
only for obligations payable under specific appropriations;
charged to, and within the limits of, each specific appropriation; and
returned to the account of advances.

relief has been granted or may be granted later to a disbursing official or agent of the department operating under an account of advances and under a law having no provision for removing charges outstanding in an account of advances; or

children.label: A
children.text: outstanding in the account of advances of the department for 2 complete fiscal years; and

children.label: B
children.text: certified by the head of the department as uncollectable.

children.label: c
children.text: Subsection (b) does not affect the financial liability of a disbursing official or agent.
